subject: Ecology
title: Regenerative Soil Practices
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Regenerative Soil Practices.

hook.text: Farmers are learning to rebuild soil by copying the way nature builds it—from the top down.

buildup.text: No-till farming leaves soil undisturbed, preserving structure, fungi, and stored carbon.

discovery.text: Cover crops protect bare soil from erosion, add organic matter, and feed microbial life.

twist.text: Regenerative farms can increase soil organic matter by 1% in five years—a huge carbon gain.

climax.text: Healthy soil grows more food with fewer inputs, making regenerative farming both green and profitable.

punchline.text: Feed the soil and the soil feeds you back.

quiz.question: How does no-till farming help soil?
options: It preserves structure, fungi, and stored carbon
It compacts soil for better drainage
